coverage: 100% of reachable node types; Object Info now preserves its As Instance component so the final wrapper switch matches Blender.
validation: All six authored input cases match the recovered-Pixels.ttf Blender reference exactly at 161722 vertices, 88994 polygon faces, and rounded local bounds. Mesh to Curve preserves attached-loop closing edges by repeating their branch pole as Blender does. Realize Instances now excludes the VM-private __font_sample_stride marker, preventing a downstream Fill Curve from dissolving already-realized glyph samples a second time while retaining authored curve attributes. The complete 538,800-index surface uses flat.nodes. Browser evaluation preserves 158,950 black and 2,772 gray POINT-domain col values; all 105,172 vertices referenced by faces retain power=1, while 56,550 non-surface points retain power=0. The interactive viewport can expose the exact 3,226 curve points / 2,797 guide segments, but authored comparison hides those non-rendering lines while preserving their evaluated bounds for Blender-equivalent camera framing. This raises surface-mask IoU from 0.0289 to 0.207180 and reduces corner RMSE from 151.9 to 8.89 pixels. The remaining low overlap and luminance correlation are genuine flat.nodes renderer and vertical-frame residuals, not a geometry-count mismatch.
